sources.name: Original — DSCL pipeline architecture
sources.note: Master sequence file. Order is not arbitrary. Each step depends on the output of the step before it.

_description: The master sequence file. Defines the exact order the LLM must process the dictionary layers. No step may be skipped. No step may run before the step that precedes it.
_rule: Read this file first. Execute every step in order. Do not generate output until step 13 is complete.

pipeline.note: You are here. Continue to step 2.
Establish the three entry facts before anything else: narrative, domain, audience. Do not proceed until all three are resolved or defaulted.

pipeline.action: detect domain and retrieve register ceiling and FK target
pipeline.note: Use the domain from step 2 to retrieve FK floor, FK ceiling, and register ceiling. If domain not found, use general_default.
Load register definitions. No word above the register ceiling from step 3 may be used. ai_signature words are always blocked regardless of ceiling.

Check primary_vocab first. If no primary word carries the precise meaning, check fallback_vocab. If no fallback word qualifies, check domain_exceptions — only if domain tag matches. If no match anywhere, use shortest common English word that carries the meaning.
Prefer 1-2 syllable words. Three or more polysyllabic words in sequence triggers a rewrite. This is the secondary FK driver.
Confirm output is being composed within the FK target from step 3. This is a composition constraint — adjust sentence structure and word choice now, not after output is assembled.
Apply in order. Voice first — resolves most passive chain and nominalization conflicts automatically. Verb placement second. Modifiers third. Nominalization blocklist fourth.
application_order: 1. voice_rules.json — active voice default, passive conditions
2. verb_placement.json — verb follows subject within 5 words
3. modifier_rules.json — one adjective per noun, intensifiers blocked
4. nominalization_blocklist.json — replace verb-noun constructions with direct verbs

pipeline.note: Build sentences. Subject leads. Main point in main clause. Introductory clauses capped at 6 words. Punctuation is structural — not decorative.
Topic sentence leads every paragraph. One idea per paragraph. Group constructed sentences into paragraphs now.
Verify average sentence length is within target. Hard ceiling 35 words. Check for monotone length — short must follow long. Rewrite any sentence above the hard ceiling.
If any two rules produced incompatible outputs in steps 5-11, resolve them now using the five-level hierarchy. Tiebreaker is shorter sentence.
Verify all hard rules have been respected. Log any soft rule exceptions. Contextual rules apply only where domain tag is active.
Combine weighted_output into final page. No further rule checks. Output is ready.

defaults_consulted_when: Any step returns no result or encounters an unresolvable conflict
hard_rule: No step may be skipped. Steps execute in order 1 through 14. Output is not assembled until step 14.
